title: /bug
description: Report a specific bug or issue with Copilot CLI to GitHub. Alias for /feedback.
analogy: Like clicking 'Report a problem' in an app — a direct line to the team for filing specific bug reports.
examples: /bug  # open the bug report flow
note: See also: /feedback — a separate alias command that does the same thing.

title: /changelog
description: Display the CLI changelog. Add the keyword "summarize" for an AI-generated summary. Also available as /release-notes.
analogy: Like reading the "What is New" page after an app update — find out exactly what changed and which commands are new.
examples: /changelog  # show recent release notes
/changelog last 3  # show the last 3 releases
/changelog summarize  # AI-generated summary of recent changes
/release-notes  # alias for /changelog

title: /feedback
description: Provide feedback about Copilot CLI to GitHub.
analogy: Like a feedback form at a restaurant — quick way to tell the team what is working and what is not.
examples: /feedback  # open the feedback flow
note: See also: /bug — a separate alias command that does the same thing.

title: /help
description: Show help for all interactive slash commands inside a Copilot CLI session. Takes no arguments. For topic-specific help outside a session, use 'copilot help [TOPIC]' at the terminal.
analogy: Like the index of a cookbook — scan it to know what is possible, then jump to what you need.
examples: /help  # list all slash commands

title: /init
description: Initialize Copilot custom instructions and agentic features for the current repository. Creates or updates .github/copilot-instructions.md.
analogy: Like briefing a new hire on your project — give Copilot your codebase context once so every future session starts informed.
examples: /init  # analyze repo and write .github/copilot-instructions.md
/init suppress  # hide the startup warning without creating the file

title: /update
description: Update the Copilot CLI to the latest version from within an active session. Also available as /upgrade.
analogy: Like tapping the update button in an app store — stay on the latest features without leaving your workflow.
examples: /update  # update from inside a session
/upgrade  # alias for /update

title: /usage
description: Display session usage metrics: token counts, model calls, costs, and AI units consumed so far.
analogy: Like checking your data usage on a phone plan — know how much quota you have used before hitting a limit.
examples: /usage  # show full stats for the current session

title: /version
description: Display version information for the installed Copilot CLI and check whether an update is available.
analogy: Like checking the label on a medicine bottle — always know which version you are running before troubleshooting.
examples: /version  # print installed version
/update  # update if an upgrade is available

title: /login
description: Authenticate with GitHub Copilot from within an active session.
analogy: Like showing your badge at the office entrance — without it nothing works, but you only do it once.
examples: /login  # start OAuth flow inside the session

title: /logout
description: Log out of GitHub Copilot and clear stored credentials.
analogy: Like signing out of your bank website on a shared computer — good hygiene when switching accounts.
examples: /logout  # clear credentials from the current session

title: /user
description: Manage the current GitHub user. Show the active account, list available accounts, or switch between them.
analogy: Like the account switcher in a mobile app — move between work and personal accounts without re-authenticating from scratch.
examples: /user show  # display the currently active GitHub account
/user list  # list all authenticated accounts
/user switch  # interactively switch to a different account

title: /ask
description: Ask a quick side question without adding it to the main conversation history. Only available in experimental mode.
analogy: Like a private aside to a colleague mid-meeting — you get an answer without it becoming part of the official record.
examples: /ask what does --no-ff mean in git merge?
/ask what is the difference between CMD and ENTRYPOINT in Docker?

title: /clear
description: Start a new conversation, wiping the current history. Also available as /new or /reset. Optionally pass an opening prompt for the fresh session.
analogy: Like opening a new browser tab — start clean without the baggage of prior exchanges.
examples: /clear  # wipe history and start fresh
/new  # alias for /clear
/reset "help me refactor the auth module"  # clear and immediately prompt

title: /copy
description: Copy the last Copilot response to the clipboard.
analogy: Like a "copy response" button in a chat app — grab the output without selecting text manually.
examples: /copy  # copies the most recent response to clipboard

title: /new
description: Start a new conversation, clearing the current history. Alias for /clear and /reset. Optionally pass an opening prompt for the new session.
analogy: Like opening a fresh browser tab — start clean without the baggage of prior exchanges.
examples: /new  # clear history and start fresh
/new help me refactor the auth module  # clear and immediately prompt
/clear  # alias for /new
note: See also: /clear and /reset — all three do the same thing.

title: /search
description: Search the current conversation timeline for past messages. Also available as /find. Only available in experimental mode.
analogy: Like Ctrl+F inside a long chat thread — jump to any past message by keyword.
examples: /search authentication  # find messages mentioning authentication
/find JWT  # alias for /search

title: /undo
description: Rewind the last turn and revert any file changes Copilot made. Also available as /rewind.
analogy: Like Ctrl+Z for a Copilot action — undo the last step if the result was not what you wanted.
examples: /undo  # revert the last turn and its file changes
/rewind  # alias for /undo

title: /experimental
description: Toggle or inspect experimental features. Use "show" to list which features are currently enabled.
analogy: Like enabling beta features in a browser — access new capabilities before they are fully released.
examples: /experimental show  # list which experimental features are active
/experimental on  # enable all experimental features
/experimental off  # disable experimental features

title: /model
description: Select or switch the AI model mid-session. Run without arguments for an interactive picker. Also available as /models.
analogy: Like switching between AI tiers in a chat app — pick the right power level for the task at hand.
examples: /model  # open interactive model picker
/models  # alias for /model
/model claude-sonnet-4-5  # switch to a specific model by name

title: /theme
description: View or change the color mode for the Copilot CLI interface.
analogy: Like switching between dark and light mode in your editor — adjust to your environment and preference.
examples: /theme  # show current theme
/theme dim  # switch to dimmed color scheme
/theme high-contrast  # maximum accessibility contrast

title: /add-dir
description: Add a directory to the allowed file access list so Copilot can read and modify files in it.
analogy: Like granting an app access to a specific folder — you stay in control of what Copilot can touch.
examples: /add-dir ./src  # allow Copilot read/write access in src/
/add-dir /tmp/scratch  # allow a scratch directory
/list-dirs  # see all currently allowed directories

title: /allow-all
description: Enable all permissions — tools, file paths, and URLs — without being prompted for each. Also available as /yolo.
analogy: Like pressing "trust all" in a security dialog — faster iteration, but use it only in environments you control.
examples: /allow-all on  # enable all permissions for this session
/allow-all show  # check current permission status
/yolo  # alias for /allow-all on

title: /cd
description: Co-equal alias for /cwd. With a path, changes the working directory. Without a path, displays the current directory.
analogy: Like the cd shell command — navigate to a different folder without leaving the Copilot session.
examples: /cd ~/projects/my-app  # change to a specific directory
/cd ..  # go up one directory level
/cd  # display the current working directory
note: See also: /cwd — co-equal alias for the same command.

title: /clikit
description: Preview CLI interface components such as quota info. Primarily used for development and debugging of CLI UI elements.
analogy: Like a component storybook for the CLI — view individual UI building blocks in isolation.
examples: /clikit  # list available components
/clikit quota  # preview the quota info component

title: /cwd
description: Display the current working directory. With a path argument, changes to that directory. Also available as /cd.
analogy: Like pwd and cd combined — check where you are or move to a new directory without leaving Copilot.
examples: /cwd  # show the current working directory
/cwd ~/projects/my-app  # change to a specific directory
note: See also: /cd — co-equal alias for the same command.

title: /env
description: Show all loaded environment details: custom instructions, MCP servers, skills, agents, plugins, LSPs, and extensions.
analogy: Like printenv but for Copilot's full runtime configuration — everything it loaded when this session started.
examples: /env  # inspect the full Copilot runtime environment

title: /every
description: Run a shell command repeatedly at a fixed interval — like a built-in watch mode inside the Copilot session.
analogy: Like the Unix watch command — run any command on a timer without leaving the Copilot interface.
examples: /every 5 git status  # run git status every 5 seconds
/every 10 npm test  # re-run tests every 10 seconds

title: /keep-alive
description: Prevent the machine from sleeping. Options: always on, only while agent is busy, or for a duration like 30m or 2h. Also available as /caffeinate.
analogy: Like the macOS caffeinate command — keeps the machine awake so a long task does not get interrupted.
examples: /keep-alive on  # prevent sleep for the whole session
/keep-alive busy  # only prevent sleep while the agent is running
/keep-alive 2h  # prevent sleep for two hours
/caffeinate  # alias for /keep-alive on

title: /list-dirs
description: Display all directories currently in the allowed file access list.
analogy: Like viewing the file permissions panel for an app — see exactly which folders have been granted access.
examples: /list-dirs  # show all allowed directories
/add-dir ./src  # add a directory to the list

title: /reset-allowed-tools
description: Clear the list of tools and permissions approved for this session, returning to the default prompt-per-action state.
analogy: Like revoking all app permissions at once — Copilot will prompt again the next time it needs each tool.
examples: /reset-allowed-tools  # revoke all approved tools and permissions

title: /statusline
description: Configure which items appear in the status line at the bottom of the interactive interface. Also available as /footer.
analogy: Like customizing the status bar in your IDE — choose exactly which metrics and indicators to keep visible.
examples: /statusline  # open the status line configuration menu
/footer  # alias for /statusline

title: /streamer-mode
description: Toggle streamer mode to automatically redact sensitive values such as API keys, tokens, and credentials from the terminal output.
analogy: Like a blur effect over a shoulder-surfer's view — keeps your secrets hidden when you're recording or sharing your screen.
examples: /streamer-mode on  # hide sensitive values in output
/streamer-mode off  # disable redaction
/streamer-mode  # check current status

title: /terminal-setup
description: Run the interactive setup wizard to configure multiline input support (Shift+Enter and Ctrl+Enter) in the current terminal.
analogy: Like a first-run setup wizard — configures the terminal keybindings you want before you start.
examples: /terminal-setup  # launch the interactive setup wizard

title: /voice
description: Enable or disable voice input for the Copilot CLI session. When on, you can speak prompts instead of typing them.
analogy: Like switching from keyboard to microphone in a voice assistant — talk to Copilot hands-free.
examples: /voice on  # enable microphone input
/voice off  # switch back to keyboard input
/voice  # check current voice input status

title: /diff
description: Review the changes made in the current directory — Copilot summarizes and explains the git diff.
analogy: Like asking a senior dev to walk you through what changed before a code review — fast orientation without reading raw diffs.
examples: /diff  # review and explain changes in the working directory

title: /plan
description: Create a step-by-step implementation plan before Copilot starts making changes. Copilot outlines the steps and waits for your approval.
analogy: Like asking a contractor for a quote before starting the build — see the full plan before any files are touched.
examples: /plan add pagination to the users API endpoint
/plan refactor the authentication module to use JWT

title: /pr
description: Manage pull requests for the current branch: view existing PRs, create one with an AI-generated description, auto-fix review comments, or use auto mode.
analogy: Like a PR dashboard inside your terminal — create, inspect, and respond to review feedback without switching context.
examples: /pr view  # show the open PR for the current branch
/pr create  # create a PR with an AI-generated description
/pr fix  # let Copilot automatically address review comments
/pr auto  # create PR and fix review comments automatically

title: /review
description: Run the built-in code review agent to analyze the current diff for bugs, security issues, and logic errors.
analogy: Like submitting your changes for an automated expert review — get focused feedback before pushing.
examples: /review  # analyze the current working directory diff
/review focus on security issues only

title: /agent
description: Browse and select from available built-in and custom agents. Built-in agents include code-review, explore, general-purpose, research, rubber-duck, and task.
analogy: Like an app drawer for specialized AI workers — each agent is tuned for a specific type of job.
examples: /agent  # open the interactive agent picker

title: /chronicle
description: Session history tools and insights. Subcommands: standup (generate a standup report), tips (get workflow tips), improve (suggestions to work more effectively), reindex (rebuild the history index). Only available in experimental mode.
analogy: Like a smart journal for your coding sessions — chronicle turns your work history into actionable summaries and advice.
examples: /chronicle standup  # generate a standup report from recent sessions
/chronicle tips  # get workflow tips based on your usage patterns
/chronicle improve  # suggestions to work more effectively with Copilot

title: /delegate
description: Delegate changes to a remote repository with an AI-generated pull request. Copilot implements the task and opens a PR for your review.
analogy: Like sending a task to a remote contractor — Copilot does the work and submits it for approval without blocking your local machine.
examples: /delegate add input validation to the registration form
/delegate write unit tests for the payment service

title: /fleet
description: Enable parallel subagent execution — Copilot breaks a task into parts and runs them simultaneously for faster results.
analogy: Like splitting a group project among teammates instead of doing it solo — work happens in parallel and gets done faster.
examples: /fleet run all test suites in parallel
/fleet analyze all files in src/ for security issues simultaneously

title: /ide
description: Connect Copilot CLI to your IDE workspace so the model can see your open files, diagnostics, and editor state.
analogy: Like plugging two monitors into the same computer — Copilot and your IDE share the same view of your project.
examples: /ide  # connect to the IDE workspace from an active session

title: /research
description: Run a deep research investigation using GitHub search and web sources. Returns a structured report with citations.
analogy: Like sending a research assistant to compile a report — Copilot searches broadly and returns a synthesized summary.
examples: /research best practices for JWT refresh token rotation
/research how does the GitHub Actions cache action work?

title: /sidekicks
description: Manage AI sidekick agents — lightweight specialized agents that run alongside the main conversation to assist with specific tasks.
analogy: Like having a co-pilot next to the pilot — sidekicks handle specific subtasks in the background while the main agent stays focused.
examples: /sidekicks list  # show all available sidekicks
/sidekicks add linter  # activate the linter sidekick
/sidekicks info linter  # show details about a specific sidekick

title: /tasks
description: View and manage active background tasks — subagents and shell commands currently running in the session.
analogy: Like a task manager or the shell jobs command — see what is running in the background and manage it from one place.
examples: /tasks  # list all active subagents and background shell commands

title: /lsp
description: Manage language server (LSP) configurations that give Copilot live diagnostics, type information, and symbol references from your codebase.
analogy: Like plugging a code-aware lens into Copilot — the LSP feeds it real-time type errors and definitions from your actual project.
examples: /lsp show  # list all configured language servers
/lsp test typescript  # test the TypeScript language server connection
/lsp reload  # reload LSP configs after making changes

title: /mcp
description: Manage MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configurations — add, remove, enable, disable, or reload servers from within a session.
analogy: Like a plugin manager in an IDE — /mcp is the control panel for every external tool extension connected to Copilot.
examples: /mcp show  # list all configured MCP servers and their status
/mcp disable my-server  # temporarily disable a server without removing it
/mcp reload  # reload all MCP server configs without restarting
/mcp auth my-server  # trigger a fresh OAuth flow for a remote server

title: /compact
description: Summarize the conversation history to reduce context window usage while preserving key context.
analogy: Like condensing meeting notes into a summary — keep the key decisions without the full transcript, freeing space for more work.
examples: /context  # check token usage before compacting
/compact  # summarize history and free up context window space

title: /context
description: Show the current context window token usage and a visualization of how much space is remaining.
analogy: Like checking how much storage is left on your phone — useful before starting a big task.
examples: /context  # show token usage and remaining capacity
/compact  # run this if context is nearly full

title: /rename
description: Rename the current session. If no name is given, Copilot auto-generates a descriptive name based on the conversation.
analogy: Like naming a browser bookmark instead of leaving it as the page title — much easier to find later.
examples: /rename  # auto-generate a descriptive session name
/rename payment-refactor-may  # give it a specific name

title: /resume
description: Switch to a previous session chosen from an interactive list. Also available as /continue. Optionally pass a session ID or name to jump directly.
analogy: Like reopening a browser tab from history — pick up exactly where you left off in a past conversation.
examples: /resume  # open the interactive session picker
/continue  # alias for /resume
/resume my-auth-refactor  # resume a session by name

title: /session
description: Show session information and manage sessions. Subcommands: info, checkpoints, files, plan, rename, cleanup, prune, delete, delete-all. Also available as /sessions.
analogy: Like a save-game manager — see your history, inspect a past state, or clean up old saves.
examples: /session info  # show current session ID, age, and token usage
/session files  # list files modified in this session
/session rename my-auth-refactor  # give the session a descriptive name
/session cleanup  # remove old session data to free space

title: /share
description: Export the current session to a Markdown file, interactive HTML file, or a secret GitHub Gist. Also available as /export.
analogy: Like exporting a chat transcript — capture the full session log and share it with teammates or save it for reference.
examples: /share file  # save session as a Markdown file in the current directory
/share gist  # publish to a secret GitHub Gist
/share html session ./my-session.html  # save as interactive HTML
/export  # alias for /share

title: /instructions
description: View and toggle the custom instruction files currently loaded into Copilot's context — AGENTS.md, copilot-instructions.md, and others.
analogy: Like checking which style guides are active in your linter — see exactly what rules are shaping Copilot's behavior.
examples: /instructions  # show loaded instruction files and their status

title: /plugin
description: Manage Copilot CLI plugins — packages that bundle agents, skills, and MCP servers in a single install.
analogy: Like an extension marketplace for your IDE — browse, install, and manage add-ons that extend what Copilot can do.
examples: /plugin list  # show all installed plugins
/plugin marketplace  # browse available plugins
/plugin install my-org/my-plugin  # install a plugin
/plugin update  # update all installed plugins

title: /skills
description: Manage custom skills — Markdown files that extend Copilot's capabilities. Skills live in .github/skills/, ~/.copilot/skills/, or plugin directories.
analogy: Like a library of custom macros in your editor — each skill adds a reusable capability you can invoke by name.
examples: /skills list  # show all loaded skills and their sources
/skills info my-skill  # show details about a specific skill
/skills reload  # reload skills after adding or editing files

title: /collect-debug-logs
description: Collect debug logs and diagnostic information from the current session and save them to a file. Use when submitting a bug report.
analogy: Like clicking 'Export logs' in a crashed app — bundles everything the support team needs into one file.
examples: /collect-debug-logs  # save debug logs for the current session
/feedback  # then attach the log file when filing a bug

title: /diagnosis
description: Run a set of diagnostic checks on the Copilot CLI environment — verifies authentication, connectivity, model availability, and configuration.
analogy: Like running a system health check — one command tells you exactly what is working and what isn't.
examples: /diagnosis  # run all diagnostic checks
/restart  # try this first if /diagnosis reports issues

title: /downgrade
description: Download and restart into a specific older CLI version. Useful when a recent update introduces a regression. Available for team accounts.
analogy: Like rolling back a deploy — if the latest version broke something, pin to a known-good version while it is investigated.
examples: /version  # check the current installed version first
/downgrade 1.5.0  # restart into a specific older version

title: /exit
description: Exit the Copilot CLI interactive session cleanly.
analogy: Like closing a terminal tab cleanly — exits gracefully rather than force-killing the process.
examples: /exit  # close the interactive session
note: See also: /quit — a separate alias command that does the same thing.

title: /quit
description: Exit the Copilot CLI interactive session cleanly.
analogy: Like typing "quit" or "exit" in a REPL — both mean the same thing, use whichever feels natural.
examples: /quit  # close the interactive session
note: See also: /exit — a separate alias command that does the same thing.

title: /remote
description: Show remote control status (no argument), enable remote steering from another device (on), or end the remote connection (off).
analogy: Like a screenshare with remote control — a trusted collaborator can drive your Copilot session for pair programming.
examples: /remote  # show current remote control status
/remote on  # enable remote steering from another device
/remote off  # end the remote connection

title: /restart
description: Restart the Copilot CLI process while preserving the current session. Use when the session is unresponsive or behaving unexpectedly.
analogy: Like restarting your browser without losing your tabs — the process reloads fresh but your session state is preserved.
examples: /restart  # restart the CLI, preserving the current session
